completed_tasks.name: README rewrite — reposition around persistent cache + agent workflows
completed_tasks.details: Hero rewritten: 'Persistent structural context and ultra-fast repeated analysis for AI coding agents.' Benchmarks (17x/9x Keycloak/Broadleaf) promoted above quickstart. New 'Cache system' section: L1/L2 layers, invalidation model, cache status/warm/clear commands. New 'Agent workflow patterns' section: continuous loop, delta context, PR review, CI. 'Java/Spring analyzer' framing moved down to command reference. 'What changes at 0.3s vs 2.7s' section added to explain agent infrastructure angle.

completed_tasks.name: CLI --help rewrite — cache central, cold/warm latency, cache commands block
completed_tasks.details: New tagline: 'Persistent structural context and ultra-fast repeated analysis for AI coding agents.' Cold/warm latency numbers added. Cache commands block (cache status/warm/clear) added prominently before Examples.

completed_tasks.name: pyproject.toml description + version bump 1.32.7 → 1.33.0
completed_tasks.details: description updated to match new positioning. version bumped to 1.33.0.

remaining_tasks.name: Live E2E test: sync workspace → pipx + verify all cache commands
remaining_tasks.details: Run bash /private/tmp/sync_to_pipx.sh, then: sourcecode cache status /private/tmp/spring-petclinic; sourcecode cache clear /private/tmp/keycloak --yes; sourcecode /private/tmp/keycloak --compact (cold); sourcecode /private/tmp/keycloak --compact (warm — should be < 0.6s). Requires normal Terminal (EPERM in Claude Code shell).

remaining_tasks.name: BUG-C1 — validate async write actually reduces latency
remaining_tasks.details: E2E benchmarks: cold run time with keycloak/broadleaf before/after 1.33.0. Async background write (daemon thread + atexit join 5s) added in 1.32.4. Verify no corruption and latency improvement.

remaining_tasks.name: Publish 1.33.0 to PyPI / Homebrew tap
remaining_tasks.details: Version bumped and committed. Needs PyPI publish and Homebrew tap update.

human_actions_pending.context: Claude Code shell has EPERM on workspace file renames. sync_to_pipx.sh uses cp to push workspace source to active pipx install.

human_actions_pending.context: Untracked since session 29. No action needed to unblock.

decisions.decision: Cache is the product moat — README and CLI help now lead with this
decisions.rationale: Benchmarks (10.5s → 0.6s Keycloak, 2.7s → 0.3s Broadleaf) change sourcecode from an occasional tool to constant agent infrastructure. All positioning now reflects this.

decisions.decision: No separate landing page — README doubles as landing on PyPI/GitHub
decisions.rationale: No HTML landing page exists in the repo. README rewrite covers both.

decisions.decision: README benchmark numbers: 17x/9x (not 33x/13x from older README)
decisions.rationale: Used more conservative rounded figures: 10.5s/0.6s = ~17x for Keycloak, 2.7s/0.3s = ~9x for Broadleaf. Previous README had different raw numbers.

decisions.decision: upgrade_hint added as a separate JSON key (not embedded in message) — from session 32
decisions.rationale: CI/MCP consumers should extract the activation command programmatically.

decisions.decision: Free-tier node cap enforced after merge_graphs in cli.py (not in GraphAnalyzer)
decisions.rationale: Policy layer separation — GraphAnalyzer stays license-unaware.

next_action: Run bash /private/tmp/sync_to_pipx.sh then verify: sourcecode cache status, warm run < 0.6s on keycloak. Then consider publishing 1.33.0.
context_notes: Session 33 was purely product/positioning work. Cache is now the central identity. All changes committed in 24df36a. No test changes needed — no logic changed. Pre-existing uncommitted files (prepare_context.py, ris.py, HANDOFF.json from session 32) still need attention but are not blocking.
